revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:函館護国神社]] {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Hakodate gokokujinja.JPG | map_type = Japan | coordinates = | map_size = | map_alt = | map_relief = | map_caption = | location = | festival = | consecration_year = | type = ''[[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]]''
(Formerly ''[[Shokonsha]]'') | leadership = | website = | architecture_style = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | name = Hakodate Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Hakodate Gokoku Shrine in May 2024.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (Worship Hall) {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|41|45|33.3|N|140|42|47.8|E}}|frame-latitude=41.764|frame-longitude=140.718}} | location = {{ill|Hakodate|en|Hakodate|qid=Q26418|lt=Hakodate, Hokkaido|1=Hakodate}}, Hokkaido, Japan | latitude = 41.75925 | longitude = 140.71328 | deities = War dead and related spirits | founded = 1869 (Meiji 2) | festival = May 11 | structure = | type = Formerly designated {{ill|Gokoku shrines|en|Gokoku shrines|qid=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine|1=Gokoku Shrines}} | map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=41|lat_min=45|lat_sec=33.3|lon_deg=140|lon_min=42|lon_sec=47.8|label=函館
護国神社|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} {{ill|Hakodate Gokoku Shrine|simple|ja|函館護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakodate Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Hakodate|en|Hakodate|qid=Q26418|lt=Hakodate|1=Hakodate}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It is a [[Gokoku Shrines|Gokoku Shrine]], or a shrine dedicated to war dead.{{Efn|Is this [[WP:BLUE]] considering that the shrine is literally named as such?}} Such shrines were made to serve to enshrine the war dead, and they were all considered "branches" of {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|en|Yasukuni Shrine|qid=Q242803|lt=Yasukuni Shrine|1=Yasukuni Shrine}}. They were renamed from {{ill|Shōkonsha|en|Shōkonsha|qid=Q11497810|lt=Shokonsha|1=Shōkonsha}} in 1939.{{Cite journal |last=TAKAYAMA |first=K. PETER |date=1990 |title=Enshrinement and Persistency of Japanese Religion |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/23917081 |journal=Journal of Church and State |volume=32 |issue=3 |pages=527–547 |jstor=23917081 |issn=0021-969X}} It was first made by the [[Matsumae Domain]] for those who died in the [[:en:Boshin_war|Boshin war]] and it has a graveyard of 50 soldiers in it..{{Cite book |last=Hardacre |first=Helen |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_Q81DQAAQBAJ |title=Shinto: A History |date=2017 |publisher=Oxford University Press |isbn=978-0-19-062171-1 |language=en}}{{rp|394}} '''Hakodate Gokoku Shrine''' ({{nihongo|函館護国神社|1=Hakodate Gokoku Shrine|Hakodate Gokoku Jinja}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Hakodate|en|Hakodate|qid=Q26418|lt=Hakodate, Hokkaido|1=Hakodate}}, Japan. It is a former officially designated {{ill|Gokoku shrines|en|Gokoku shrines|qid=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine|1=Gokoku Shrines}} established in 1869 following the {{ill|Battle of Hakodate|en|Battle of Hakodate|qid=Q716562|lt=Hakodate War|1=Battle of Hakodate}}. == History == The shrine was founded in May 1869 (Meiji 2) after the end of the Hakodate War. On May 21 of the same year, the new government held a {{nihongo|Shōkon Festival|招魂祭|1=Shōkonsai}} on {{ill|Ōmori Beach|ja|大森浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmori Beach|qq=}}, followed by the construction of a {{nihongo|Shōkon Grounds|招魂場}} at the foot of Mount Hakodate using labor from captured former shogunate soldiers.{{Cite book|和書|author=須藤隆仙|editor=太田俊穂・監修|year=1980|title=幕末維新戊辰戦争事典|pages=484-485|publisher=[[新人物往来社}} Initially referred to as the {{nihongo|Hakodate Shōkonsha|函館招魂社}}, it was renamed Hakodate Gokoku Shrine in 1939 (Showa 14) and designated as an official Gokoku Shrine.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://archives.c.fun.ac.jp/hakodateshishi/tsuusetsu_03/shishi_05-02/shishi_05-02-07-03-02.htm|title=函館市史通説編第3巻|pages=747-755|publisher=[[函館市中央図書館]]|accessdate=2015-05-07}} After the end of World War II, the shrine was renamed {{nihongo|Shiomigaoka Shrine|潮見丘神社}} in 1946 to comply with occupation policies. However, it reverted to its original name in 1955 (Showa 30).{{Cite web|和書|url=http://archives.c.fun.ac.jp/hakodateshishi/tsuusetsu_04/shishi_06-01/shishi_06-01-06-01-03.htm|title=函館市史通説編第4巻|pages=275-283|publisher=函館市中央図書館|accessdate=2015-05-07}} In recent years, the shrine has promoted itself as a site for prayers related to matchmaking.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.hakobura.jp/db/db-view/2010/04/post-48.html|title=函館市公式観光情報「はこぶら」|publisher=函館市|accessdate=2015-05-07}} == Grounds == * '''Main Shrine Building''': Built in 1942 (Showa 17). * '''Shōkon Monument''': Inscribed by {{nihongo|Shimizutani Kimitaka|清水谷公考}}. The stone, known as "Kameishi," is said to have been recovered from the sea by workers under Takadaya Kahei. * '''Tachibana Repose Monument''': Dedicated to the {{nihongo|Tachibana|橘}}, a destroyer sunk during the 1945 Hakodate air raids. * '''Government Army Cemetery''': Located behind the main shrine, with approximately 60 graves of soldiers who died in the Hakodate War. * '''Tulip Song Monument''': Commemorating the children’s song {{nihongo|Tulip|チューリップ}}. File:Gokokujinja shoukonhi.JPG|Shōkon Monument File:Gokokujunja tachibana.JPG|Tachibana Repose Monument File:Gokokujinja kangunbochi.JPG|Government Army Cemetery File:函館チューリップの歌碑.png|Tulip Song Monument == festival == The festival was initially set on June 10, later moved to May 6 in 1905 (Meiji 38), and finally to May 11 in 1913 (Taisho 2). During the early Meiji period, the festival was one of Hakodate’s most vibrant, accompanied by the {{nihongo|Shōkonsha Horse Races|招魂社競馬}} at nearby entertainment districts.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.lib-hkd.jp/hensan/hakodateshishi/tsuusetsu_02/shishi_04-11/shishi_04-11-05-03-02.htm|title=函館市史通説編第2巻|pages=1360-1362|publisher=函館市中央図書館|accessdate=2015-05-07|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140714200920/http://www.lib-hkd.jp/hensan/hakodateshishi/tsuusetsu_02/shishi_04-11/shishi_04-11-05-03-02.htm|archivedate=2014-07-14}} == Surroundings == The slope leading from the shrine to {{ill|Horaicho Station|ja|函館市電宝来町停留場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horaicho Station|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is called {{nihongo|Gokoku Shrine Slope|護国神社坂}}, formerly known as {{nihongo|Shōkon Slope|招魂坂}}. At the base of the slope stands a statue of Takadaya Kahei. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Heketsuhi Monument|ja|碧血碑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heketsuhi Monument|qq=}} * [[Controversies surrounding Yasukuni Shrine]] * {{ill|Martyrs' shrines (China)|en|Martyrs' shrines (China)|qid=Q6800982|lt=Martyr's Shrines|1=Martyrs' shrines (China)}} * {{ill|Martial temple|en|Martial temple|qid=Q11124210|lt=Martial temple|1=Martial temple}} and {{ill|Wen Wu temple|en|Wen Wu temple|qid=Q7231588|lt=Wen Wu temple|1=Wen Wu temple}} * {{ill|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|en|National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|qid=Q5956488|lt=National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine|1=National Revolutionary Martyrs' Shrine}} * {{ill|Eternal Spring Shrine|en|Eternal Spring Shrine|qid=Q4501089|lt=Eternal Spring Shrine|1=Eternal Spring Shrine}} * {{ill|Chinese Cultural Renaissance|en|Chinese Cultural Renaissance|qid=Q1073969|lt=Chinese Cultural Renaissance|1=Chinese Cultural Renaissance}} * [[Ancestral shrine]] * {{ill|Gallant Garden|en|Gallant Garden|qid=Q5518867|lt=Gallant Garden|1=Gallant Garden}} * [[Gokoku Shrines]] * {{ill|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|en|Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|qid=Q1541043|lt=Tomb of the Unknown Soldier|1=Tomb of the Unknown Soldier}} * {{ill|Arlington National Cemetery|en|Arlington National Cemetery|qid=Q216344|lt=Arlington National Cemetery|1=Arlington National Cemetery}} * {{ill|Valhalla|en|Valhalla|qid=Q177860|lt=Valhalla|1=Valhalla}} (home to the souls of fallen warriors in Scandinavian mythology) * {{ill|Walhalla (memorial)|en|Walhalla (memorial)|qid=Q321240|lt=Walhalla Shrine|1=Walhalla (memorial)}} (a hall of fame in Germany honoring "commendable and honorable Germans") * {{ill|Eternal Spring Shrine|en|Eternal Spring Shrine|qid=Q4501089|lt=Eternal Spring Shrine|1=Eternal Spring Shrine}} * {{ill|The common end of myriad good deeds|en|The common end of myriad good deeds|qid=Q9609095|lt=The common end of myriad good deeds|1=The common end of myriad good deeds}} * {{ill|Greek hero cult|en|Greek hero cult|qid=Q1878906|lt=Greek hero cult|1=Greek hero cult}} == Notes == {{Notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://hakodate-gokoku.jp/ Official Website of Hakodate Gokoku Shrine] * [http://www.hakobura.jp/db/db-view/2010/04/post-48.html Hakodate Gokoku Shrine on Hakodate City’s Tourism Website] }} {{Shinto-stub}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{translated page|ja|函館護国神社|version=101145406|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Draft categories| {{Gokoku shrines}} {{everybodywiki link|Hakodate Gokoku Shrine}} [[ja:函館護国神社]] {{Commons category|Hakodate Gokoku-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q19887675}} [[Category:Facilities Opened in 1869]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:History of Hakodate]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hakodate]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
